mnemonics.title: Remember Transformation Order
mnemonics.content: T-R-S: Translate, then Rotate, then Scale - Always think 'To Rotate Safely, Translate first' to avoid unexpected results.

mnemonics.title: Homogeneous Coordinate Trick
mnemonics.content: Think of 'H' in Homogeneous stands for 'Height' - adds one dimension to lift 2D points to 3D space for matrix operations.

mnemonics.title: Matrix Structure Memory
mnemonics.content: Affine matrix: [Rotation/Scale/Shear in top-left 2×2] [Translation in last column] [0 0 1 at bottom]

acronyms.usage: Order matters: Always apply in reverse order for desired composite transformation

visualTips.visualization: Imagine pushing an object - all points move same distance in same direction like sliding on ice
Think of a spinning wheel - all points rotate around center by same angle
Zooming an image - uniformly pulls/pushes all points from origin
Sliding top of a deck of cards horizontally - parallel lines stay parallel but distances change
Mirror image across axis - x or y coordinate gets negated

analogies.analogy: Like adding '1' to address a letter - you need apartment number (the 1) to deliver to exact location (do transformation)
Like following GPS directions - going to A then B gives different result than B then A, order matters!
Railway tracks remain parallel whether you view them from distance or angle - they never converge like roads
